data.title: Analyst Reiterates Buy on DUG, Maintains $3.20 Price Target on Strong Growth, Sector Tailwinds and Middle East Seismic Opportunity
data.description:
PAC Partners analyst Caleb Weng has maintained their bullish stance on DUG stock, giving a Buy rating on April 14.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Caleb Weng has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors including DUG’s solid recent performance and favourable industry dynamics. He notes that the company has delivered strong revenue and EBITDA growth, with robust margins, while its orderbook, though flat, is sufficient to support earnings at the upper end of FY26 market expectations.

He also highlights a strengthening demand backdrop, as major oil companies are increasing exploration budgets and sector peers signal rising imaging revenues into 2026. In addition, a sizeable upcoming seismic data project in the Middle East points to a substantial medium-term opportunity for processing and imaging work, and when combined with DUG’s reasonable FY27 valuation multiple, this underpins his conviction in maintaining a Buy rating and a $3.20 price target.

In another report released on April 14, Ord Minnett also maintained a Buy rating on the stock with a A$2.93 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk
